Author: Devrim Ozcay
Title: I Used GitHub Copilot for 6 Months and Here’s Why I’m Going Back to Manual Coding
Text:
Member-only story
I Used GitHub Copilot for 6 Months and Here’s Why I’m Going Back to Manual Coding
“This Changes Everything!”
March 2024. GitHub Copilot just dropped for free, and the whole dev Twitter was losing its collective mind.
Dave (our team lead): “Guys, we’re all getting Copilot! Productivity is about to go through the roof!”
Me: “Finally! No more Stack Overflow rabbit holes!”
Sarah: “I’m gonna code so fast, I’ll finish sprints in 2 days!”
Narrator: None of us finished sprints in 2 days.
Six months later, I’m sitting here, staring at my VS Code, and I just did something that would’ve been unthinkable back then:
I disabled GitHub Copilot.
And honestly? I feel like I just woke up from a very expensive, very disappointing dream.
Week 1: “Holy Shit, This Is Magic!”
First day with Copilot was like Christmas morning.
I typed function calculateTax
and BOOM — a complete, working tax calculation function appeared. Perfect syntax, proper edge cases, even JSDoc comments.
Me (to myself): “This is it. This is the future. I’m never writing boilerplate again.”
